description_long: This obelisk is a tall, slender monument with a sharply tapering shaft that rises from a broad, rectangular base. The base is composed of several stepped layers, giving it a solid and imposing foundation, and the shaft extends vertically in a uniform, narrow form. The surface appears to be made of dark stone or concrete, with a matte, slightly rough finish that suggests durability and weather resistance. There are no visible inscriptions or decorative carvings on the shaft or base, giving the structure a minimalist and utilitarian appearance. The tip of the obelisk is pointed and unadorned, maintaining the geometric simplicity of the overall design. The object is likely intended as a commemorative or decorative feature in an urban park or plaza.
description: A tall, dark, minimalist obelisk with a stepped rectangular base and a sharply pointed tip, made of stone or concrete. The surface is matte and slightly rough, with no visible ornamentation or inscriptions.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full height of the obelisk, with the stepped base and the narrow shaft rising vertically.
description_view_1: Side view is nearly identical to the front, emphasizing the symmetry and uniform taper of the shaft.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, with the same stepped base and pointed shaft visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the symmetry and the clean, unadorned surfaces of the obelisk.
attribute_base_design: The base is a broad, rectangular platform with multiple stepped layers, each slightly smaller than the one below, creating a tiered effect. The design is simple and robust, with no visible decorative features or engravings.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is sharply pointed and unadorned, maintaining a clean, geometric finish with no unique features or decorations.
